verses.1: My son, pay attention to my wisdom.
verses.3: For the lips of an adulteress drip honey.
verses.4: but in the end she is as bitter as wormwood,
verses.5: Her feet go down to death.
verses.6: She gives no thought to the way of life.
verses.7: Now therefore, my sons, listen to me.
verses.8: Remove your way far from her.
verses.9: lest you give your honor to others,
verses.10: lest strangers feast on your wealth,
verses.11: You will groan at your latter end,
verses.12: and say, “How I have hated instruction,
verses.13: I haven’t obeyed the voice of my teachers,
verses.14: I have come to the brink of utter ruin,
verses.15: Drink water out of your own cistern,
verses.16: Should your springs overflow in the streets,
verses.17: Let them be for yourself alone,
verses.19: A loving doe and a graceful deer—
verses.20: For why should you, my son, be captivated with an adulteress?
verses.21: For the ways of man are before the LORD’s eyes.
verses.22: The evil deeds of the wicked ensnare him.
verses.23: He will die for lack of instruction.
